scenarios.job_relevance: Practices the SCCM/MECM-style thought process of checking deployment state, agent check-in, compliance, encryption, and patch readiness before closing a new laptop imaging ticket.

findings: HIGH imaging: Endpoint imaging state requires review — Review deployment logs and confirm provisioning completed.
HIGH endpoint: Device is running an unsupported OS baseline for this demo — Plan OS upgrade or replacement before relying on the endpoint.
HIGH endpoint: Endpoint has excessive local administrator exposure — Remove unnecessary local administrator access.
HIGH endpoint: Endpoint encryption is disabled — Enable disk encryption or quarantine the device until remediated.
HIGH compliance: Endpoint patch status requires review — Confirm patch scan health and apply missing updates if needed.
MEDIUM endpoint: Device check-in is stale — Confirm ownership, network reachability, and agent health.
report.job_relevance: Practices the SCCM/MECM-style thought process of checking deployment state, agent check-in, compliance, encryption, and patch readiness before closing a new laptop imaging ticket.
technician_actions: Confirm the device record exists in endpoint inventory or SCCM/MECM.
Review deployment logs for the failed imaging or task sequence state.
Confirm the endpoint checks in successfully after remediation.
Validate encryption, compliance, and patch visibility before handoff.
Update the ticket with findings, action taken, and verification evidence.
report.ticket: User reports a newly imaged laptop is not ready for use.
report.title: Failed imaging / endpoint provisioning triage
verification_steps: Imaging state is complete or a reimage path is documented.
Device check-in is current and visible to endpoint tooling.
Encryption and compliance state are acceptable for user handoff.

scenarios.title: Failed imaging / endpoint provisioning triage

scenarios.job_relevance: Practices Active Directory and endpoint lifecycle hygiene: disabled users should not keep active device ownership without review, reclaim, or reassignment.

findings: HIGH lifecycle: Disabled user still has assigned devices — Review offboarding and reclaim or reassign the user's devices.
MEDIUM identity: User login is stale — Review account need and disable it if unused.
report.job_relevance: Practices Active Directory and endpoint lifecycle hygiene: disabled users should not keep active device ownership without review, reclaim, or reassignment.
technician_actions: Confirm the account is disabled and identify the assigned endpoint.
Check whether the device should be reclaimed, reassigned, or retired.
Remove stale ownership notes after manager or asset approval.
Document the offboarding cleanup in the ticket.
report.ticket: Offboarding review finds a disabled user still assigned to a laptop.
report.title: Disabled user still assigned to device
verification_steps: Disabled user no longer owns active endpoint assignments without review.
Device ownership and asset lifecycle status are documented.

scenarios.title: Disabled user still assigned to device

scenarios.job_relevance: Practices identity administration escalation: privileged access should have MFA and group membership should be reviewed before access is trusted.

findings: HIGH identity: Privileged user does not have MFA enabled — Require MFA for privileged endpoint or identity access.
report.job_relevance: Practices identity administration escalation: privileged access should have MFA and group membership should be reviewed before access is trusted.
technician_actions: Confirm privileged group membership and business need.
Escalate or enable MFA according to identity policy.
Review whether local or endpoint admin access is still required.
Document the identity hygiene remediation or exception.
report.ticket: Identity review flags a privileged account without MFA enabled.
report.title: Privileged identity missing MFA
verification_steps: MFA is enabled or an approved exception is recorded.
Privileged group membership has a documented owner and purpose.

scenarios.title: Privileged identity missing MFA

scenarios.job_relevance: Practices prioritizing endpoint support work by risk, evidence, and remediation steps instead of treating all device issues as equal.

report.job_relevance: Practices prioritizing endpoint support work by risk, evidence, and remediation steps instead of treating all device issues as equal.
technician_actions: Prioritize devices with the most findings and highest severity first.
Start with compliance blockers: encryption, patch visibility, and policy state.
Separate quick fixes from devices that need reimage, owner review, or escalation.
Record remediation evidence before closing endpoint tickets.
report.ticket: Endpoint support queue needs a prioritized remediation plan.
report.title: Endpoint compliance remediation queue
verification_steps: Highest-risk devices have an owner, next action, and expected resolution path.
Compliance-impacting findings are remediated or escalated.

scenarios.title: Endpoint compliance remediation queue
